frameworks.name: binocular rivalry / perceptual competition
frameworks.one_line: brief mixed-image presentations induce competition between simultaneously presented categories

frameworks.name: deep neural network as ventral-stream model
frameworks.one_line: DNN softmax outputs treated as a candidate model of human perceptual choice

core_question: Do DNN softmax predictions reproduce human perceptual dominance patterns for briefly presented mixed images?
key_assumptions: brief mixed-image presentation reveals which input wins perceptual competition
DNN softmax over categories is a reasonable proxy for ventral-stream readout
manipulations: image category mixtures (e.g. faces vs non-faces)
low-level image properties such as contrast
key_findings: humans show category-level dominance (faces dominate non-faces)
high-contrast images dominate low-contrast counterparts in human perception
DNN softmax responses only partially capture these human dominance patterns
interpretations: human-DNN divergences pinpoint visual decision-making dimensions current ventral-stream DNNs miss
